Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about EV insurance and road tax in Malaysia

Yes — EV insurance premiums are typically 10% to 30% higher than comparable petrol cars. The main reasons are the high cost of battery replacement (RM30,000–RM80,000), specialised workshop requirements, and limited availability of EV parts in Malaysia. However, you can offset this with a strong NCD and by comparing insurers on PolicyStreet to find the most competitive rate.
Not automatically under all policies. Most comprehensive plans cover battery damage caused by accidents, fire, or flooding — but coverage varies significantly between insurers. Some treat the battery as a separate component with its own terms and sub-limits. Always confirm that your policy explicitly covers battery damage before purchasing. PolicyStreet compares policies across 20+ insurers so you can see exactly what is and isn't covered.
Starting January 2026, EV road tax in Malaysia is calculated based on motor output in kilowatts (kW) — not engine displacement (cc) like petrol cars. The scale ranges from RM20–RM70 for EVs up to 100 kW, up to RM20,000 for EVs above 1,010 kW. Most popular EVs (BYD, Tesla Model 3/Y, Proton e.MAS 7) fall in the RM55–RM350 range — still significantly cheaper than equivalent petrol cars.
Yes, significantly cheaper for most EV owners. A petrol SUV with a 2,000cc engine pays around RM900–RM1,500 per year in road tax. A comparable EV like the BYD Atto 3 (150 kW) pays approximately RM171 per year. The savings on road tax alone often offset a portion of the higher insurance premium. Use our calculator above to see your exact road tax estimate.
NCD stands for No-Claim Discount — a reward for not making any insurance claims in a policy year. In Malaysia, NCD starts at 0% for new policyholders and increases each year: 25% (Year 2), 30% (Year 3), 38.33% (Year 4), 45% (Year 5), and up to 55% from Year 6 onwards. At 55% NCD, you effectively pay almost half your base premium. It is the single biggest discount available on your car insurance.
For EVs, comprehensive insurance is strongly recommended. Given the high replacement cost of EV batteries (RM30,000–RM80,000) and the specialised repair requirements, third party coverage leaves you exposed to significant out-of-pocket costs if your vehicle is damaged. Comprehensive insurance covers your own vehicle damage, theft, fire, and flooding — all risks that are especially costly for EV owners.
Yes — flood damage is a higher risk for EVs than petrol cars. EV batteries are sensitive to water ingress. In a flood situation, water damage to an EV battery pack can result in a total loss worth tens of thousands of ringgit. Standard comprehensive policies do not automatically include flood coverage in Malaysia — you need to add Special Perils as an add-on. Given Malaysia's flood-prone climate, this add-on is highly recommended for all EV owners.
Yes, you can. In Malaysia, NCD is attached to the vehicle owner, not the vehicle. If you are switching from a petrol car to an EV, you can transfer your existing NCD entitlement to your new EV policy — provided the vehicle ownership remains in your name and you renew within the eligible period. This is one of the best ways to reduce your EV insurance cost from day one.
Drive+ is PolicyStreet's membership programme that gives you road tax discounts when you renew with us. There are three tiers: Lite (RM39.90/year, RM50 road tax discount), Standard (RM69.90/year, RM90 discount), and Premium (RM99.90/year, RM120 discount). For most EV owners, the Standard or Premium tier pays for itself — you get back more in road tax savings than the membership costs. Use the road tax calculator above to see exactly which tier gives you the best net saving for your car.
Likely yes over time. As more EVs enter the market, more workshops become EV-certified, and insurers accumulate more claims data, premiums are expected to become more competitive. However, the July 2026 CBU import rules (minimum RM200,000 CIF value, minimum 180 kW motor output for fully imported EVs) may push new CBU EV prices higher in the short term, which could keep premiums elevated for premium models. Locally assembled EVs like the Proton e.MAS 7 are less affected.

Why EV Insurance is Different

Electric vehicles have unique considerations that affect your coverage and cost.

EV-Specific Coverage

  • Battery pack protection (most expensive component)
  • Charging cable & equipment
  • Electric motor damage
  • Charging station liability

Road Tax — EV vs Petrol

Petrol cars are taxed by engine cc (can be RM1,000–2,000+). EVs are taxed by motor output kW — typically RM200–RM600 for most models. Big savings!

How NCD Works

Every claim-free year earns you more NCD — up to 55% off your premium. New EV owners start at 0%, but loyal no-claim drivers save the most.

Why Compare?

EV insurance prices vary significantly across insurers. PolicyStreet compares 20+ insurers in one go so you always get the best price for your EV.
